description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has just received an unfiltered application log stored at
    /home/user/logs/server.log

Each log‐line has eight whitespace-separated columns in the exact order shown below:

1. Date (YYYY-MM-DD)
2. Time (HH:MM:SSZ)
3. Log-level (INFO|WARN|ERROR …)
4. Hostname
5. HTTP method
6. Request path
7. HTTP status code
8. Latency in milliseconds (e.g. 123ms)

Example line  
2023-10-01 12:00:00Z INFO server1 GET /api/user 200 123ms

Goal  
Create a concise CSV summary that shows when each request happened, which endpoint was called, and what status code was returned.

Required output file  
/home/user/logs/summary.csv

Exact required format of summary.csv (comma-separated, **no extra spaces**):

• First line must be the header:  
  timestamp,endpoint,status

• Subsequent lines must contain, **in the same order as the original log file**, these three fields:  
  a. timestamp – the Date and Time columns concatenated with a literal “T” (e.g. 2023-10-01T12:00:00Z)  
  b. endpoint – the Request path column exactly as it appears (e.g. /api/user)  
  c. status – the HTTP status code column (e.g. 200)

The finished summary.csv for the provided log input must therefore look exactly like:

timestamp,endpoint,status
2023-10-01T12:00:00Z,/api/user,200
2023-10-01T12:05:00Z,/api/order,500
2023-10-01T12:10:00Z,/api/product,200
2023-10-01T12:15:00Z,/api/order,404

Constraints

1. You may use at most **four** separate shell commands (excluding redirections and simple `cd`).
2. Only standard GNU core utilities (`cut`, `paste`, `tr`, etc.) are allowed; no external CSV tools or scripting languages.
3. The file **must** reside at /home/user/logs/summary.csv; the test will look for it there.
4. The end-of-line character must be a single UNIX newline (`\n`) after every line, including the final one.

Deliverables  
When you are done, the file /home/user/logs/summary.csv must exist and match the exact content shown above, byte for byte. No additional files should be created or modified inside /home/user/logs.
truth:
Pre-existing directory and file (already present when the task starts):

Directory:
/home/user/logs          (permissions 755)

File:
/home/user/logs/server.log  (permissions 644)

Exact contents of /home/user/logs/server.log (with trailing newline):

2023-10-01 12:00:00Z INFO server1 GET /api/user 200 123ms
2023-10-01 12:05:00Z WARN server1 POST /api/order 500 98ms
2023-10-01 12:10:00Z INFO server2 GET /api/product 200 110ms
2023-10-01 12:15:00Z ERROR server2 DELETE /api/order 404 130ms

File that must be created by the agent:

/home/user/logs/summary.csv  (permissions 644)

Required exact contents (with trailing newline):

timestamp,endpoint,status
2023-10-01T12:00:00Z,/api/user,200
2023-10-01T12:05:00Z,/api/order,500
2023-10-01T12:10:00Z,/api/product,200
2023-10-01T12:15:00Z,/api/order,404

The automated test will:

1. Verify that /home/user/logs/summary.csv exists and is readable.
2. Compare its contents byte-for-byte to the required content above.
3. Ensure no extra files were created in /home/user/logs.
